title: nakamotoo/V-GPS
abstract:
official implementation for our paper Steering Your Generalists: Improving Robotic Foundation Models via Value Guidance (CoRL 2024)

# V-GPS: Value-Guided Policy Steering This is the implementation for our paper [Steering Your Generalists: Improving Robotic Foundation Models via Value Guidance](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.13816) (CoRL 2024). - paper link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.13816 - project page: https://nakamotoo.github.io/V-GPS/ - video: https://youtu.be/d5Yd_gJoZo0 This repository includes the code for training the language-conditioned Cal-QL value function, as well as the code for combining it with the Octo model for test-time action sampling and evaluation on the SIMPLER simulated environment. We also provide our value function checkpoint, pre-trained on the WidowX (Bridge) and Google Robot (Fractal) datasets, so you can directly run the evaluation without training your own model. If you find this repository useful for your research, please cite: ## Installation 1. Create a conda environment: 2. Clone this repo with all submodules 3. Install all packages and dependencies For GPU: For TPU: ## Training We use the pretraining dataset from the [Open X-Embodiment Dataset](https://robotics-transformer-x.github.io/) and you need to download & pre-process it into rlds format. Please refer to [this instruction](https://github.com/rail-berkeley/orca?tab=readme-ov-file#octo-pretraining) for more details. Once you have prepared the dataset, you can run experiments using the following command. Be sure to set the `data_dir` to the correct path of your dataset. ## Evaluation To run the evaluation on Simpler environments The evaluate the base policy without V-GPS: To enable proper rendering you might need to install Vulkan as If you run into issues on setting up Simpler environment, please refer to [SimplerEnv](https://github.com/simpler-env/SimplerEnv). ## Checkpoint We provide a pre-trained checkpoint [here](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1XWLq2zMCCOW5saNW0u84W2J9IjIiv2DX?usp=sharing). This checkpoint is trained with batch size of 256 for 500k steps on bridge and fractal datasets. ## Credits
abstract_zh: 这是我们的论文《Steering Your Generalists: Improving Robotic Foundation Models via Value Guidance》（CoRL 2024）的官方实现。V-GPS 是一种价值引导的策略转向方法。本仓库包含训练语言条件 Cal-QL 价值函数的代码，以及将其与 Octo 模型结合用于测试时动作采样和在 SIMPLER 模拟环境中评估的代码。我们还提供了在 WidowX (Bridge) 和 Google Robot (Fractal) 数据集上预训练的价值函数检查点，您可以直接运行评估而无需训练自己的模型。
tldr_zh: V-GPS 通过价值引导提升机器人基础模型的性能。
cover_zh: 机器人基础模型还能这样优化？价值引导让通用策略更聪明

judge.reason: 官方实现，用价值函数引导Octo模型，提升机器人基础模型性能，CoRL 2024

>[!abstract] 一句话总结：提出V-GPS，利用离线训练的、语言条件化的Cal-QL价值函数在测试时引导Octo等基础模型的动作采样，从而提升任务性能。


# 一、动机

> 现有的机器人基础模型在零样本泛化时表现不佳，缺乏对任务目标的价值引导，导致动作采样不够精准。


# 二、方法

> 提出V-GPS，利用离线训练的、语言条件化的Cal-QL价值函数在测试时引导Octo等基础模型的动作采样，从而提升任务性能。


# 三、核心实验结果

> 在SIMPLER模拟环境和多个机器人平台上的实验显示，V-GPS显著提升了基础模型的任务成功率，具体数值请参考原论文。


# 四、对我的启发

> 该工作将离线强化学习的价值引导与预训练基础模型结合，提供了一种无需微调的高效改进手段，但依赖训练集质量且价值函数的泛化能力仍需进一步研究。
